title: Contributions of cortical feedback to sensory processing in primary visual cortex

frameworks.name: cortical feedback
frameworks.one_line: top-down projections shape V1 responses beyond feedforward sensory drive

frameworks.name: structure-function mapping
frameworks.one_line: linking anatomical feedback projections to functional V1 modulation

frameworks.name: internal brain states
frameworks.one_line: V1 activity reflects ongoing state, not only stimulus drive

core_question: How does cortical feedback to V1 shape sensory processing beyond what feedforward inputs predict?
key_assumptions: feedback projections to V1 are anatomically quantifiable
textbook feedforward accounts under-describe V1 response complexity
key_findings: V1 responses are more complex than textbook feedforward descriptions suggest
V1 local activity reflects both sensory input and internal brain states
interpretations: feedback function in V1 is poorly understood relative to anatomy
internal state contributes to V1 activity alongside sensory processing
limitations_noted: understanding of feedback function is rudimentary compared to its quantified anatomy
